content:
- All required lanes passed for story-10 task 01: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`.
- Marked the task and plan completed, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, and confirmed the long-lane support split kept `clippy -D warnings` green while preserving both ignored contracts.
- Next after any context cut: stage only the story-10 code + Ralph files (not the unrelated `.agents/skills/info-about-repo/SKILL.md` deletion or the unrelated story-15 draft), commit with test evidence, push, and stop.

- Read previous progress, refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, and inspected chooser rules plus active task files.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks and no visible `blocked_by`/priority gates on the remaining active tasks. Inspected the actual repo stack: Rust 2024 workspace with `runner`, `source-bootstrap`, and a real ignored long-lane e2e harness under `crates/runner/tests/support/e2e_harness.rs`.
- Chosen next task should be `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/02-task-e2e-fk-heavy-initial-scan-and-live-catchup.md` because story 10 already has the baseline harness in place and this next FK-heavy e2e slice most directly validates the core helper-shadow + reconcile architecture before documentation/reporting tasks.
- Next after quitting: write that task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stop.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill docs.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/02-task-e2e-fk-heavy-initial-scan-and-live-catchup.md` and I am now checking whether it is in planning (`TO BE VERIFIED`) or execution (`NOW EXECUTE`) state.
- Next after quitting immediately due to context limit: continue from the task file state, either create/update the plan file and stop, or execute the approved plan with TDD if the task says `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Read the active task body and confirmed it is still pre-plan: there is no `<plan>` entry and no `TO BE VERIFIED`/`NOW EXECUTE` marker yet.
- Inspected existing Ralph conventions across `.ralph/tasks`; task markdown stores a `<plan>` path, and plan files themselves end with the execution-state marker.
- Next after quitting immediately due to context limit: mirror the story-10 plan style, add a new plan path into the active task, write the FK-heavy e2e plan with TDD slices plus one boundary-cleanup target, leave it at `TO BE VERIFIED`, and stop.

- Read the closest story-10 example plan plus current long-lane support, baseline E2E test, reconcile contracts, and the design/test-strategy docs that call out FK-safe initial scan plus live catch-up.
- Confirmed the current long-lane coverage only proves a single-table baseline path; the missing boundary is a multi-table FK graph on the real CDC path while destination PK/FK constraints stay enabled.
- Next after quitting immediately due to context limit: write the new story-10 task plan around a parent/child/grandchild real-database scenario, add the `<plan>` path into the task file, leave the plan marked for verification/execution according to confidence, and stop.

- Added the active task plan path to `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/02-task-e2e-fk-heavy-initial-scan-and-live-catchup.md`.
- Wrote `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/02-task-e2e-fk-heavy-initial-scan-and-live-catchup_plans/2026-04-19-fk-heavy-e2e-plan.md` using `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries`, anchored on a real parent/child/grandchild CDC scenario and a harness refactor that removes baseline-only assumptions from shared long-lane support.
- Marked the new plan `NOW EXECUTE` and quit immediately as required. Next turn should read the plan, execute it with red-green TDD slices, and continue until `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` are all green or the design proves wrong and the plan is switched back to `TO BE VERIFIED`.

- Read previous Ralph progress, confirmed the active task path, and reloaded the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill docs.
- Confirmed the previous turn already created a plan and marked it `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn should read the task + plan and execute in red-green slices unless the design proves wrong.
- Next: inspect the active task markdown and its linked plan, then implement the first failing test slice and continue through the full check/test lanes.

- Read the active task and execution plan, confirmed it is in `NOW EXECUTE`, and inspected the existing long-lane harness plus verify/bootstrap contracts.
- Found the main boundary smell exactly as planned: `crates/runner/tests/support/e2e_harness.rs` hard-codes a one-table customers scenario into shared Docker/bootstrap/verify orchestration.
- Next: create the first RED slice by adding a new ignored FK-heavy long-lane test against a generic harness interface, run that single test to capture the failure, then refactor shared support just enough to make it pass before extending behavior.

- Replaced the baseline-only long-lane support with a reusable `CdcE2eHarness`, kept the default scenario as a thin wrapper, and added the FK-heavy long-lane contract in the shared long-lane integration crate.
- Verified the new FK-heavy scenario with real parent/child/grandchild tables, helper-shadow initial scan, live catch-up mutations, repeated reconcile stability, constraint preservation, and final `runner verify` on real tables only.
- Next: run formatting if needed, then the required full lanes in order (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`), fix anything they expose, and only after all pass update the Ralph task state.
